mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with rate declines.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and imminent recession risks, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction during documented recession onset periods.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative 60-day returns in > 60% of cases, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and output, which translates to positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days exclusively during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 20-day returns during stable rate environments, or the predictive power reverses sign during tightening cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth falling below 1.0 percent indicates severe economic contraction, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in > 50% of instances when GDP growth drops below the 1.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a declining federal funds rate and rising total nonfarm payrolls creates a favorable liquidity and employment environment, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in > 55% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated consumer price inflation combined with contracting industrial production signals stagflationary pressures, forcing central banks to tighten policy and causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances, or the mechanism fails to predict returns when inflation is driven by transitory supply shocks.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in retail sales reflect robust consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth shows zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC < 0.01.
